description: Nine-run incidence-noise robustness scan on the no-distance combinatorial graph-shell branch. The packet update rule and graph-shell kernel are frozen while the line-graph incidence structure is perturbed by controlled rewiring and delete-insert operators.

kernel_mode_family.label: graph-shell
kernel_mode_family.definition: W_ij depends only on graph-shell distance 1,2,3 on the perturbed edge-incidence graph, with no coordinate distance and no Gaussian embedding.

architecture_notice: This branch varies only the combinatorial incidence structure of the explicit edge-graph control operator. Packet initialization, projected-transverse state sector, graph size, and shell-weight kernel family remain frozen.
baseline_notice: The current C0.1-C0.2 combinatorial branch does not yet contain a clean braid_like_exchange baseline. C0.3 therefore anchors to the nearest exchange-like protected surrogate, counter_propagating_corridor, and treats braid detection as an allowed outcome rather than an assumed starting point.

base_seed_reference.selection_note: same frozen packet family as C0.1-C0.2

resolution_policy.followup_gate: Promote only if a braid_like_exchange class appears reproducibly or if the smeared-transfer surrogate shows a sharp incidence-noise threshold.

representatives.notes: Nearest exchange-like protected surrogate from C0.1-C0.2.

perturbations.label: Ultra-weak rewiring
perturbations.notes: Very small random endpoint rewiring at fixed edge count.

perturbations.label: Weak rewiring
perturbations.notes: Weak random endpoint rewiring at fixed edge count.

perturbations.label: Moderate rewiring
perturbations.notes: Moderate random endpoint rewiring at fixed edge count.

perturbations.label: Moderate deletion-insertion
perturbations.notes: Moderate delete plus insert perturbation at fixed edge count.

perturbations.label: Clustered rewiring patch
perturbations.notes: Localized rewiring inside a BFS patch on the incidence graph.

perturbations.label: Random localized defect pair
perturbations.notes: Two localized defect neighborhoods perturbed without disconnecting the graph.

perturbations.label: Degree-biased perturbation
perturbations.notes: Incidence rewiring biased toward high-degree targets.

perturbations.label: Connectivity-edge stress test
perturbations.notes: Perturb edges with low shared-neighbor support while preserving connectedness.
